rationale: Most schema/resolver contracts check out: config tests pass, the /tmp-built CLI resolves the Goal protocol to 9/6/6/12 with raw thresholds, unknown model ids stay advisory, substitutes marshal as {}, PanelGateKeys order is respected by rendering, and the recorded panel gate is decision-inert. I found one R4 validation miss: explicit empty model values are accepted when family is also present, because the typed struct cannot distinguish absent model from model: "". R4 explicitly lists an empty-string model as a Load refusal, so this is a schema/type-correctness gap.
concrete_changes_required: Make Load refuse an explicit empty-string reviewer model even when family is present, for both global panel.reviewers and per-gate panel.gates.<gate>.reviewers. Add a regression covering model: "", family: codex.

findings.summary: R4 requires an explicit empty-string model to be refused, but validateReviewerEntries accepts model: "" when family is also present.
findings.details: Spec R4 says Load must refuse a reviewer entry with neither model nor family, or an empty-string model. validateReviewerEntries only checks r.model() == ""; r.model() falls back to Family when Model is empty, so the explicit-empty-model case is silently accepted and resolved as the legacy family.
findings.repro: With /tmp-built binary: config containing panel.gates.bead.reviewers: [{model: "", family: codex, count: 1}, {model: claude-sonnet-5, count: 1}] exits 0 and emits slot R1 model codex. Expected: Load error with recovery line.
